title: [REDACTED], and overall experience is good, but ….
text:
1) Mandate (AutoPay) Limits  
Currently, AutoPay mandate is quite restrictive:  
- UPI mandate is limited (generally up to ₹1–2 lakh depending on bank)  
- Net banking mandate allows only up to around ₹5 lakh  
- Default practical usage often stays near ₹1 lakh for seamless transactions  

Because of this, it becomes very difficult for serious investors to invest large lump sum amounts.

For example:  
- If I want to invest ₹10 lakh or more in one go during market correction, it is not possible smoothly using mandate  
- I have to split transactions or use manual payment, which defeats the purpose of AutoPay  

Other platforms like NJ Wealth provide higher mandate flexibility (₹20–50 lakh or more), which is much more practical for high-value investors.

👉 Request:  
Please introduce higher mandate limits (₹20–50 lakh or customizable based on [REDACTED] and bank approval), especially via net banking/eMandate.

---

2) Limited SIP Frequency Options  
Currently, SIP options are limited to monthly (and some dates).  

👉 Missing features:  
- Daily SIP option  
- Weekly SIP option  

These are very useful for:  
- Better cost averaging  
- Investing during volatile markets  
- More flexible cash flow management  

Many investors prefer daily/weekly SIP to capture market movements more effectively.

---

✅ Overall:  
Groww is a very user-friendly and excellent platform, but these limitations make it less suitable for high-value and advanced investors.

Improving mandate limits and adding flexible SIP frequencies will significantly enhance the platform experience.

title: [REDACTED] interface
text:
Groww is an excellent trading and investment app, especially for beginners as well as experienced investors. The app interface is extremely clean, simple, and user-friendly, making investing stress-free. Opening an account is quick, and everything from stocks, mutual funds, ETFs, IPOs, and F&O is available on one platform.

What I really like is the transparent pricing with zero AMC, fast order execution, and detailed stock & mutual fund information that helps in making informed decisions. The app runs smoothly without lag, and fund transfer & withdrawals are quick and hassle-free.

Customer support is responsive, and regular updates keep improving the experience. Overall, Groww has made investing easy, safe, and convenient. Highly recommended for anyone serious about growing their wealth 📈

title: Easy to use App
text: I’ve been using this app for last 4 years.  It’s a wonderful app with very [REDACTED] UI. Frequent updates makes the app even better. A must try app for all those who are interested in investments.

title: Simple, Smart & Actually Helpful for Beginners
text:
Ive been using Groww for a while now, and honestly, its one of the easiest investing apps Ive come across. The interface is super clean and not confusing at all, which really helped me when I was just starting out with mutual funds and stocks.
What I like most is how straightforward everything feels — from KYC to making your first investment, the whole process is smooth and doesnt feel overwhelming. The app also provides basic explanations and insights, which are useful if you’re still learning the ropes.
That said, theres still room for improvement. Sometimes the app can lag a bit during peak market hours, and I wish they had more advanced tools for experienced investors. But for beginners and even intermediate users, it does the job really well.
Overall, Groww is a sol[REDACTED] if you want a no-nonsense, user-friendly platform to start your investment journey without getting lost in complicated features.

title: Interface for ipads
text:
The app really needs a proper interface designed specifically for iPad. Right now, it is quite difficult and uncomfortable to use on a larger screen. The layout does not feel optimized, and many elements seem stretched or poorly aligned, which affects usability.

Navigation can also be confusing at times, and simple tasks take more effort than they should. An iPad-friendly interface with better spacing, improved responsiveness, and optimized controls would make a big difference in the overall [REDACTED].

I hope the developers consider adding a dedicated iPad interface in future updates, as it would greatly improve accessibility and ease of use.

title: Happy Investor
text: Finally found an app that makes SIPs feel effortless, you can start with just 500. The UI is incredibly intuitive—I was able to complete my KYC and start my first fund in under 10 minutes. Plus, zero commission on direct funds means more money stays in my pocket. Highly recommend for anyone starting their wealth journey.

title: iPad Version Performance & UI Issues
text:
I would like to share my feedback regarding the iPad version of the Groww app. While the platform is efficient and user-friendly on mobile devices, the experience on iPad currently requires improvement.

Firstly, the app interface does not appear to be fully optimized for iPad dimensions. The layout feels stretched and misaligned, which affects usability and overall visual appeal. A dedicated iPad-responsive design would significantly enhance the experience.

Secondly, there are noticeable performance issues with charts. The charts tend to lag during interaction, especially while scrolling, zooming, or switching timeframes. This delay can hinder effective analysis, particularly for active traders who rely on real-time responsiveness.

These issues impact both usability and efficiency. I hope the development team considers optimizing the app for iPad screens and improving chart performance in upcoming updates.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

title: Great Platform, But Where is the iPad App?
text:
Groww offers one of the most intuitive and clean interfaces for investing, but it is becoming increasingly difficult to overlook the lack of a native iPadOS application.

While the iPhone app is excellent, using it on an iPad feels like a massive step backward. Currently, iPad users are forced to use the scaled-up iPhone version, which results in:

Wasted Space: Massive black bars on the sides and a layout that doesn't utilize the extra screen real estate for multi-chart viewing or deeper data analysis.

Inconvenience: Most major competitors already offer dedicated, high-resolution iPad apps that allow for a much more professional "terminal" feel.

For a platform that prides itself on technology and [REDACTED], it’s disappointing that power users are stuck with a mobile-only layout. Navigating complex portfolios or analyzing candlesticks is far more convenient on a tablet, but only if the app is designed for it.

Bottom line: Please bring a dedicated iPad app to the ecosystem. Until then, it’s hard to call this a complete cross-platform solution.

title: I first started my stock investment and SIP in this app only from 2023
text:
I began my stock market investment journey and SIPs with the Groww trading app in 2023, and it has been a great experience so far. As a beginner, I found the app extremely user-friendly and easy to understand. The clean interface, simple navigation, and clear explanations made investing feel less intimidating.

Starting SIPs and investing in stocks was smooth, and tracking my portfolio has been very convenient. Over time, I have been able to accumulate funds consistently, and I’m really happy to see that my investments are growing steadily. The app provides helpful insights and updates that keep me informed about my investments.

Overall, Groww is a reliable and beginner-friendly app for anyone who wants to start their investment journey with confidence. I’m satisfied with my experience and happy with the progress of my funds.

title: This app sometime do fraud
text:
I bought shares of Dee Development Engineers Ltd and sold them at a profit of ₹1,500. After that, I noticed that another share was automatically purchased within one minute at a higher price through the app, which immediately resulted in a loss. I quickly exited the position and booked a loss of ₹1,300.
Sometimes the app buys shares automatically without my intention. When I contacted customer support, they said that the order was executed from my end and then closed the chat.
